weather.summary: Saturday: 12°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 13°C, clear sky

metadata.greeting: This weekend in London is packed with exciting and engaging activities perfectly suited for a curious 3-year-old! From free museum adventures with dinosaurs and space exploration to outdoor explorations in city farms and Easter egg hunts, there's something for everyone, and the weather looks promising for a wonderful weekend out and about.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, drop-in, and suitable for a wide age range, with books to engage a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central
transport.tip: Check term times before heading out!

activities.title: Young V&A (Museum of Childhood)
activities.description: Interactive galleries, including Design, Imagine and Play, with over 2000 toys. Mini Museum for under 2s offers sensory experiences.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for children, with interactive exhibits and a sensory space for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: The closest tube station is not accessible; consider alternative transport if you have a buggy.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for toddlers, with a safe and engaging play environment.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line or Overground to Stratford
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play spaces known as Story Worlds and the Story Garden playground. Baby Storytelling, crafts, and singalong sessions.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers immersive play and storytelling sessions specifically designed for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line or Overground to Stratford
transport.tip: Check the schedule for Baby Storytelling sessions.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Farm animals, cafe, fresh veggies. A great family day out.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to see farm animals and enjoy the outdoors in an urban environment.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses serve the area
transport.tip: Check the website for special events.

activities.title: Mudchute City Farm
activities.description: One of the largest urban farms in London with a wide range of animals and activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of animals and space to run around, plus free weekend play sessions.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Mudchute
transport.tip: Check for free weekend play sessions.

activities.title: East Side Play - Pop Up Adventure Playground
activities.description: Pop-up adventure playground sessions where children can build, create, and play freely with various materials.
activities.whyItWorks: Encourages creativity and active play in a safe, supervised environment. Perfect for energetic kids.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to All Saints or Poplar, plus local buses
transport.tip: Check their website or social media for locations and times.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Learn about London's history as a port. Don't miss Mudlarks, an interactive soft play area.
activities.whyItWorks: The Mudlarks soft play area is perfect for toddlers, and the museum is designed with child-friendly exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to West India Quay or Canary Wharf
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, volcanoes, and fascinating creatures. Check out the earthquake simulator!
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs! Enough said. Plus, it's free and there are interactive exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Go early to beat the crowds; check for family-friendly events.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works! The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for younger children.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden is specifically designed for young children and offers hands-on experiences with science.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the website for show times and special events.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Natural history museum with an aquarium, butterfly house and gardens with views over London.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of indoor and outdoor activities, including animals, gardens, and a hands-on natural history museum.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill
transport.tip: Check the website for family events.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo in Hampstead Heath, home to rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: It's free and features animals like wallabies and lemurs.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Northern Line to Golders Green, then a bus or walk.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.description: Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover facts about Cutty Sark's voyages and unscramble a nautical code for a treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An Easter adventure exploring a historic ship with a reward at the end.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Explore maritime history with child-friendly activities and exhibits. Features the All Hands Children's Gallery.
activities.whyItWorks: The All Hands Children's Gallery is designed for kids, and there are often Saturday 'Character Encounters'.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark
transport.tip: Check for special events and character encounters.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: Large playground with a Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and sensory trail.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a large play space with a pirate ship, sensory trail and beach area, plus it's free.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Lancaster Gate or Queensway.
transport.tip: Can get crowded, arrive early!

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm Easter Activities
activities.description: Participate in free easter activities at Vauxhall City Farm
activities.whyItWorks: Enjoy family fun with farm animals

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Victoria Line to Vauxhall
transport.tip: Check their website for locations and times.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Embark on an Easter adventure, searching for Lindt Gold Bunny statues in the gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: The hunt for bunnies offers a fun outdoor activity with a chocolate prize at the end.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance; the event is very popular!

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for giant Easter eggs hidden around the park.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a visit to the zoo with an Easter egg hunt for a fun and engaging day.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Northern Line to Camden Town, then a bus.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Easter Egg-Stravaganza
activities.description: Beatrix Potter live show, egg hunt, easter bonnet parade, face painting, arts and crafts, café and plenty of delicious treats!
activities.whyItWorks: Live show, egg hunt, easter bonnet parade, face painting, arts and crafts

transport.fromHackney: Train from Waterloo to Putney
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Hobbledown Heath Easter Egg'splorers
activities.description: Themed egg trail, indoor/outdoor play, animal meets, and an 'Impossible Eggspedition'.
activities.whyItWorks: Indoor/outdoor play, animal meets, and an egg trail.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Piccadilly Line to Hounslow West, then a bus.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at Southbank Centre
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity with shows, musical adventures, puppetry, and free activities.
activities.whyItWorks: A wide variety of activities, including many free options.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then Jubilee Line to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the schedule and book in advance.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: A theatrical production of Alice in Wonderland.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic story brought to life on stage.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Piccadilly Line to Hammersmith.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Octonauts Above and Beyond
activities.description: An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive trail, invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: District Line to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Whipsnade Zoo's Easter Trail
activities.description: Easter trail and egg hunt with animal-themed activities and treats.
activities.whyItWorks: Animal-themed activities and treats

transport.fromHackney: Train from St Pancras to Harpenden and then a bus
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.
